macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Dips to $73,717 Amid Market Correction
items.summary: Bitcoin's price stood at $73,717.11 at 9 a.m. ET on March 17, 2026, down $165 from yesterday and reflecting a failure to hold above the $74,450 resistance level. Ethereum traded at $2,317.10, XRP at $1.50, and Tether at $1.00.

items.title: Bitcoin Fails to Sustain $74,450 Resistance, Eyes Key Support Levels
items.summary: Bitcoin slipped below $74,450 after failing to maintain gains, renewing bearish sentiment with immediate support at $69,378-$71,840 and deeper support at $61,530-$64,560. Analysts note choppy price action characteristic of a counter-trend advance.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $74K with Bullish Signals from ETFs and Geopolitics
items.summary: Bitcoin returned to around $74,000, potentially eyeing $80,000 if it maintains the level, driven by BlackRock Bitcoin ETF inflows for six days and US-Iran de-escalation. AI mining competition emerges as a concern but deemed manageable.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Multiplicity: The Curious Case of Arbitrariness in Machine Learning Wins AAAI-26 Educational Video Award
items.summary: The video 'Multiplicity: The Curious Case of Arbitrariness in Machine Learning' was announced as the winner of the AAAI-26 Educational Video competition on March 17, 2026, highlighting issues of arbitrariness in ML models.

items.title: Niv-AI Exits Stealth to Optimize GPU Power Efficiency in Data Centers
items.summary: Niv-AI emerged from stealth on March 17, 2026, with technology using rack-level sensors and AI models to predict and synchronize power loads on GPUs, aiming to unlock more capacity in existing data centers without new builds.

items.title: University of Minnesota Develops Knowledge-Guided AI for Revolutionizing Flood Forecasting
items.summary: New studies published on March 17, 2026, introduce a hybrid knowledge-guided machine learning model that outperforms traditional methods in predicting streamflow and flood levels by automatically learning from watershed data.

items.title: UB Researchers Evaluate AI Models for Wildfire Spread Prediction
items.summary: University at Buffalo study on March 17, 2026, finds deep learning models like ConvLSTM improve wildfire spread forecasts but cannot yet replace physics-based tools like FARSITE, which achieved higher precision and F1 scores.

items.title: Reinforcement Learning Emerges as Core of AI's Shift to Reasoning and Action
items.summary: Opinion piece on March 17, 2026, argues RL drives the 'second act' of AI, enabling inference scaling for better enterprise problem-solving, with examples from Walmart and Nestlé using RL for simulations.

items.title: Rise of AI-Driven Prioritization Tools in Enterprises for 2026
items.summary: Article on March 17, 2026, discusses how prescriptive AI prioritization outperforms manual models by integrating multi-dimensional data for faster, unbiased decisions, with tools like Monday.com and PagerDuty leading domain-specific applications.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Pokémon Pokopia Becomes One of 2026's Most Popular Games
items.summary: Nintendo's latest Pokémon game, which blends life simulation, town-building, and sandbox elements, has surpassed 2.2 million in global sales within four days of launch, establishing itself as a major hit for the year.

items.title: Raw Fury CEO Pim Holfve Appointed as New CEO of Parent Company Combined Effect
items.summary: Pim Holfve, who led Raw Fury to 43% revenue growth in 2025 through successful releases like Blue Prince and ROUTINE, has been promoted to CEO of Combined Effect while continuing to oversee Raw Fury. He plans to position the company as an enabler for indie game success.

items.title: Limited Run Games Announces Renewed Fan-First Strategy
items.summary: Limited Run Games, a premium publisher of physical games, announced a renewed commitment to a fan-first focus on March 17, 2026.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: 5G Stocks to Watch: EchoStar, KT, and Mobix Labs Highlighted
items.summary: MarketBeat identifies EchoStar, KT, and Mobix Labs as key 5G stocks to follow on March 17, 2026, amid focus on wireless connectivity and semiconductor solutions for 5G applications.[1]

items.title: Collective Metals Launches Inaugural Drilling at Rocas Uranium Project
items.summary: Collective Metals (CLLMF) began diamond drilling on March 16-17, 2026, at the Rocas Uranium Project, targeting 1,200-1,500 meters across 6-8 holes for shallow high-grade uranium mineralization.[6]
